source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
On January 22, 2015, a magnitude 4.2 earthquake occurred in the Ili Kazakh Autonomous Prefecture of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region. It is known that the propagation speeds of the P-wave and S-wave near the surface during the earthquake are $9.1\mathrm{km/s}$ and $3.7\mathrm{km/s}$, respectively. At the epicenter observation station, the time difference between the arrival of the P-wave and S-wave from the source was recorded as $5.4\mathrm{s}$.

1. Find the distance from this observation station to the seismic source.
2. What the observatory first observed was vertical oscillation or horizontal shaking?

image_dependency: Question is fully specified by text with no explicit reference to diagrams, charts, or visual attributes
multi_step: The question has two sub-questions: compute source distance and identify whether the first motion is vertical or horizontal
Distance finding requires combining the P-wave speed, S-wave speed, and arrival-time difference
The second part requires using the fact that P-waves arrive first and relating P-wave behavior to vertical/compressional motion
answer_text contains two distinct outputs, indicating more than a single lookup or direct extraction step
verifiability: The numeric answer can be checked from the provided wave speeds and time difference using a standard travel-time relation
The qualitative answer can be checked against the known property that P-waves are observed first and are compressional/longitudinal

decision_reasons: Question and answer assets are both present, with substantial question text and no registry issues.
The problem is fully text-specified and has no image dependency, so core semantics are intact.
Initial scoring indicates strong verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value.
Risk flags are manageable follow-up concerns rather than evidence of missing core semantics.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
On January 22, 2015, a magnitude 4.2 earthquake occurred in the Ili Kazakh Autonomous Prefecture of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region. It is known that the propagation speeds of the P-wave and S-wave near the surface during the earthquake are 9.1 km/s and 3.7 km/s, respectively. At the epicenter observation station, the time difference between the arrival of the P-wave and S-wave from the source was recorded as 5.4 s.

1. Find the distance from this observation station to the seismic source.
2. What the observatory first observed was vertical oscillation or horizontal shaking?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text:
34 km
Vertical oscillation (up and down vibration)
normalization_notes: Kept the original two-part question structure.
Lightly standardized units and answer phrasing without changing semantics.
